items.prompt: For generating combinations without reusing earlier indices, which state is essential?

options.text: The next candidate start index.
Only the length of the global result list.
A boolean saying whether the input is sorted.
The total number of recursive calls made so far.

feedback.reason: A start boundary records which candidate indices remain legal for the current combination.
feedback.details: The current path alone may not distinguish whether earlier indices are still available, so the candidate boundary belongs in state.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.last_output_length: Global result count does not constrain the next candidate range.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sorted_flag: Sorting may be relevant elsewhere but does not replace the start boundary.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.recursion_count: Call count does not define legal candidates.

items.prompt: For generating permutations of n distinct values, which state prevents reusing the same input position within one path?

options.text: A used-by-index set, boolean array, or bitmask.
Only a monotonically increasing start index.
Only the number of completed permutations.
Only the previously chosen value.

feedback.reason: Permutation state must distinguish every input position already selected in the current branch.
feedback.details: A used structure supports arbitrary next-index order while preventing repetition of the same position.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.start_only: A start index would prevent permutations that use a lower index later.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.result_count: Global output count does not identify branch-local availability.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.previous_value: One value cannot represent all used positions.

items.prompt: A target-sum backtracking solver uses only positive candidate values. Which state can avoid recomputing the sum of the current path?

options.text: The remaining target after chosen values are subtracted.
The maximum input value.
Only recursion depth.
The number of solutions already found.

feedback.reason: Remaining target is a sufficient summary of the path sum for this constraint.
feedback.details: It supports completion at zero and sound positive-only overshoot pruning without scanning the full path.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.global_max: The maximum does not summarize current progress.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.call_depth_only: Different choices at the same depth can have different sums.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.result_size: Output count does not describe branch feasibility.

items.prompt: A grid word-search call is represented by (row, col, wordIndex). What additional branch-local state is required when cells cannot be reused in the same path?

options.text: Which cells are already used by the current path.
The number of words found in earlier searches.
The sum of values in the current row.
Only the grid dimensions.

feedback.reason: The call coordinates and target position do not encode which cells were used earlier in the branch.
feedback.details: Visited state can be represented explicitly or by reversible in-place marking, but it must be path-local.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.global_result_count: Earlier output count does not prevent current-path reuse.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.row_sum: Row aggregates do not encode used cells.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.board_size_only: Dimensions support bounds checks but not path reuse constraints.

items.prompt: Which state is redundant in a combination solver when current path length can be read in O(1)?

options.text: A separate chosenCount field always equal to path.length.
The next legal candidate boundary.
The selected values.
The unresolved target for a constrained-sum problem.

feedback.reason: State that is exactly and cheaply derivable need not be duplicated.
feedback.details: Redundant mutable fields can drift out of sync and enlarge the proof burden.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.start_index: This is not derivable from path length alone.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.current_path: The output under construction is necessary.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.remaining_target: This may summarize feasibility more efficiently than recomputing the path sum.

items.prompt: A solver mutates one shared current path. Where should that path belong conceptually?

options.text: Branch-local state whose contents are restored before sibling exploration.
Immutable global configuration shared without restoration.
Only metadata about completed results.
The fixed list of all possible input candidates.

feedback.reason: The current path is mutable state describing the active root-to-node branch.
feedback.details: It may be implemented as one shared object for efficiency, but choose-recurse-unchoose semantics make it logically branch-local.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.global_configuration: The path changes across branches and cannot be treated as immutable configuration.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.result_metadata: The path represents the current partial solution.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.candidate_catalog: Candidates and selected path are different state roles.

items.prompt: A partitioning solver receives a string and a start index. Which state is sufficient to identify the unresolved suffix?

options.text: The exclusive boundary where the next segment must begin.
Every previous boundary even when the current path already stores the segments.
The number of partitions already found.
Only the final character of the previous segment.

feedback.reason: The next unprocessed position determines the remaining suffix.
feedback.details: The chosen segments remain in the path for output, while start directs candidate boundary enumeration.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.all_previous_end_indices: The full boundary history may be redundant for finding the next suffix.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.result_count: Output count does not locate the unresolved suffix.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.last_character_only: That does not identify where the remaining input begins.

items.prompt: Why is storing the entire remaining substring often less attractive than storing its start index?

options.text: It can create repeated substring copies while the index already identifies the same suffix.
Indices cannot identify positions in a string.
Substring values have no length.
Recursion always requires a copied input.

feedback.reason: A compact boundary can identify the unresolved region without duplicating immutable input data.
feedback.details: State should preserve necessary information while avoiding redundant materialization.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.indices_invalid: Indices are the standard way to identify suffix boundaries.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.recursion_requires_copy: Recursive calls can share immutable input with boundary state.

items.prompt: A solver stores both remainingTarget and the full current sum, updating both independently. What risk does this introduce?

options.text: The two redundant values can become inconsistent after an update or undo.
The branch will be pruned automatically.
Every result is necessarily duplicated.
The algorithm becomes O(1) space.

feedback.reason: Redundant mutable state increases synchronization obligations.
feedback.details: Prefer one canonical representation or derive one value from the other when the cost is acceptable.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.automatic_pruning: Redundant state does not automatically improve correctness.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.duplicate_results: Duplication is not an inevitable consequence.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.constant_space: Additional fields do not reduce recursion state.

items.prompt: Which fields can form sufficient state for k-combination generation from an indexed array? Select all that apply.

options.text: The next candidate start index.
The values or indices selected so far.
The number of values still required, when not derived from path length.
The total number of calls made across the entire search.

feedback.reason: Sufficient state must identify the partial output, legal candidate range, and remaining completion requirement.
feedback.details: Instrumentation such as global call count is not part of the search semantics.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.global_call_count: It does not affect legal completion.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.start_index: It prevents reusing earlier indices and duplicate orderings.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.remaining_slots: It can support completion and capacity pruning.

items.prompt: Which information can be represented by a used bitmask in a permutation solver? Select all that apply.

options.text: Which input positions are already selected in the current path.
Which positions remain available, by complementing within the n-bit domain.
How many positions are selected, by counting set bits.
The exact order in which selected values appear in the path.

feedback.reason: A bitmask compactly represents membership, but not the ordered path itself.
feedback.details: Permutation output still needs path order or an equivalent ordered construction.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.selected_order: A set of bits does not encode ordering.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.used_positions: Each bit can represent one input position.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.available_positions: Availability is derivable from the used mask.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.chosen_count: The count is derivable from the mask.

items.prompt: Which state components are relevant for path-based grid search? Select all that apply.

options.text: The current row and column.
The index or amount of target already matched.
Cells already used in the current path.
The number of unrelated paths found earlier.

feedback.reason: Grid-search state combines location, target progress, and branch-local reuse constraints.
feedback.details: Global output metadata does not replace those branch semantics.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.completed_result_count: It does not determine current branch legality.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.target_progress: It identifies the next required symbol.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.path_visited: It enforces no-reuse within the branch.

items.prompt: Which properties suggest that a state field is redundant? Select all that apply.

options.text: It is deterministically and cheaply derived from other state.
Every choose and undo operation must update it in lockstep with another field.
No completion, legality, pruning, or output logic reads it.
It is the only field that determines whether a candidate is legal.

feedback.reason: State should be minimal but sufficient.
feedback.details: Essential legality information cannot be removed merely to reduce field count.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.branch_legality: That makes it essential rather than redundant.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.never_read: Unused state has no semantic purpose.

items.prompt: Which state should normally be immutable configuration shared by all branches? Select all that apply.

options.text: The original candidate list when branches do not modify it.
The fixed target or constraint definition.
The mutable selected path.
The current path's visited-cell set.

feedback.reason: Shared immutable configuration is distinct from mutable branch state.
feedback.details: Keeping the distinction explicit reduces accidental cross-branch contamination.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.current_path: The path changes per branch and requires isolation or restoration.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.path_visited: Visited state is branch-local in reusable-cell path search.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.input_candidates: All branches may safely read the same immutable input.

items.prompt: Arrange the process for designing backtracking state.

elements.text: Define what a complete valid result means.
Define which next choices are legal from a partial state.
Choose the minimal fields needed to answer completion and legality questions.
Specify how every field changes and is restored for one branch.

feedback.reason: State design follows from the result and transition contracts.
feedback.details: Choosing fields before defining their semantic queries often produces redundant or insufficient state.

items.prompt: Arrange the state update for a target-sum branch using remainingTarget.

elements.text: Verify that the candidate is legal for the current state.
Subtract the chosen value from remainingTarget.
Add the value back when returning to the parent state.

feedback.reason: The child state must reflect the choice, and the parent state must be restored afterward.
feedback.details: This transition keeps remainingTarget synchronized with the current path.

items.prompt: A permutation solver stores a length-n boolean used array and a recursion path of depth at most n, excluding materialized outputs. Select the auxiliary-space complexity.
constraints: Only one depth-first branch is active at a time.

feedback.reason: The used array and active recursion path each scale linearly with n.
feedback.details: Their sum remains O(n). The potentially factorial output is excluded by the stated auxiliary-space accounting.

items.prompt: A grid path solver uses an R by C visited matrix and recursion depth at most L. Select its auxiliary-space bound, excluding the immutable grid.
constraints: The visited matrix is stored explicitly.
At most L recursive calls are active.

feedback.reason: Explicit visited state and the active recursion stack contribute separate terms.
feedback.details: If visited is instead encoded by reversible in-place board mutation, the explicit RC term may disappear, but that is a different state representation.
